intro: Drummondita longifolia, also known as the "Tangle Vine", is a climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Drummondita longifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from digestive issues to skin problems.
